Fairly values all real estate and personal property in the county — homes, land, commercial buildings and vehicles — the basis for property taxes. Provides homestead, veterans and senior exemptions plus online property records and tax maps. (304) 487-8397 Courthouse · 1501 W Main St, Princeton

Fiscal officer and custodian of official records: elections and voter registration, probate, vital records (birth, death, marriage), hunting & fishing licenses, notary and civil-service services, payroll and county budgeting. Photo ID is required to vote in WV. (304) 487-8338 Suite 121, Courthouse · Princeton

A constitutional office (WV Const. Art. 8, §9), elected to six-year terms. Registrar, recorder and custodian of all Circuit and Family Court pleadings, manages the county jury system, and assists self-represented litigants. Courthouse · Princeton

Law enforcement, service of civil process, operation of the county jail, and collection of real-estate and personal-property taxes. Blends time-honored values with modern public-safety strategies to safeguard the community. Courthouse · Princeton
Prosecutes felony and misdemeanor criminal cases on behalf of the State of West Virginia and advises county government, working closely with law enforcement and the courts. Courthouse · Princeton
The Circuit Court hears felony criminal cases and major civil matters for the county's judicial circuit. Courthouse · Princeton
Handles divorce, custody, child and spousal support, and other domestic-relations matters. Courthouse · Princeton
Handles misdemeanors, small claims, arrest and search warrants, and emergency protective orders. Magistrate Court · Princeton

The Mercer Communications Center is the Public Safety Answering & Dispatch Point for police, fire, EMS and emergency management countywide — processing 911 and non-emergency calls promptly and professionally to help save lives and protect property. Emergencies: 911
Disaster preparedness, response coordination and public-safety planning for Mercer County.

An open-admission county shelter (Director Stacy Harman) providing humane care for stray and relinquished animals. Accepts animals from county residents; all pets are spayed/neutered before adoption; rescue-friendly with 501(c)(3) partners. (304) 425-2838 · 961 Shelter Road, Princeton

Empowers residents to live healthy lives in healthy communities. Full clinic services via Blue Prince Family Health: primary and preventative care, wellness and cancer screenings, immunizations and vaccines. Clinic: Blue Prince Family Health
General-aviation airport (Manager Jim Pilkins) serving the greater Bluefield/Princeton region under the oversight of the County Commission. (304) 327-8440 · 300 Markell Dr, Ste 201, Bluefield

Drop-off recycling at the Sanitary Landfill. Hours: Mon–Fri 7 AM–5 PM, Sat 8 AM–1 PM. Accepts #1 & #2 plastics, ferrous metals & cans, all aluminum, and bagged paper / broken-down cardboard (materials must be separated). 749 Frontage Road, Princeton (Landfill)
Community-based alternative sentencing and supervision programs.
Coordination with the Southern Regional Jail and inmate services.
Supervision of adults on probation within the judicial circuit.
Supervision and services for juveniles under court jurisdiction.
Floodplain permitting and compliance with FEMA/NFIP development standards.
The county's nationally recognized, award-winning litter-control and community-cleanup initiative.
County park facilities, ballfields, pool and recreation across the county.
Promotes tourism and travel to Mercer County. visitmercercounty.com
Economic development, business recruitment and workforce initiatives.
The annual Mercer County Fair and associated community events.
